title: What ever happened to IFS Independence?
gist: A forum moderator questions whether Intel Foundry Services' 2024-promised operational independence from Intel Products has quietly eroded, observing IFS now appears to be managed to serve the broader Intel rather than as the separated, transparent entity Gelsinger described.

insights.claim: Two years after Intel promised IFS would be an independent boundary between product group and foundry, the perception is that IFS is now being managed to help the greater Intel, with Intel Products working to make the foundry successful.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Erosion of foundry independence undercuts the arms-length separation external foundry customers need; a trust signal to watch for winning third-party fab business against TSMC.
insights.quote: the feel is that IFS is being managed to help the greater Intel and Intel Products is working to make foundry succesful
insights.locator: 2 years ago, Pat and the Intel team

insights.claim: Intel's original 2024 rationale held that splitting off the foundry would give IFS greater financial transparency and flexibility and clearer separation/independence for external foundry customers and suppliers.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Establishes the benchmark against which IFS's actual independence, and its credibility with outside customers, should be judged.
insights.quote: provides our external foundry customers and suppliers with clearer separation and independence from the rest of Intel

insights.claim: Under the 2024 restructuring IFS leadership continued reporting to Intel's CEO with no leadership changes, and only a separate operating board was established, structurally limiting true independence.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: The reporting structure suggests independence was governance-light from the start, supporting skepticism that IFS operates at genuine arms length.
insights.quote: No changes have been made to the leadership team, who continue to report to Intel's CEO, however a separate operating board will be established.
